topic_text: When Current condition changes to from Weather Underground, do Blink lights using Philips Hue. Blink your Hue lights when it starts to rain outside. Every time it rains outside your Hue bulbs will blink a few times.

This is one in 4 Applets used to turn two warning lights over my coat rack on or off based on the weatherTwo Applets turn on, or off, a light based on temperatureThe other two turn on, or off, a light based on rainfall or clear skyWeather info from weather.com service is used to call a function on a Particle Photon that lights the warning lightsYou have to select the "digitalWrite" function on your Photon below and make sure that the lamp is connected to pin D1 (or other that you indicate in the action, below).

When Current condition changes to from Weather Underground, do Call a function using Particle. Spark Pixels Current Weather: IF Rain Then Blue. Use with a some Neopixels and the Spark Pixels android app: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=kc.spark.pixels.android&hl=en1Flash your Particle device with the latest Spark Pixels firmware from github: https://github.com/sparcules/Spark_Pixels2To configure the IFTTT set the "Then call (Function Name)", select the *SetMode* function for your Particle device.3 Setup the "with input (Function Input)" to *M:IFTTT WEATHER,C6:0000FF,*, where 0000FF is the hex value for BLUEAlso, don't forget the comma at the end of the color.4Sit back and wait for the weather to change :)
